findingsNew.claim: code-graph-context.ts resolves and imports the Memory MCP compiled weighted-walk module at top level before SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_SEEDED_PPR_RANKING is checked, but the referenced dist/lib/graph/bfs-traversal.js artifact is absent from this checkout.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked .opencode/skills/**/bfs-traversal.* inventory; source files exist, but the required system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/lib/graph/bfs-traversal.js path does not. Checked that seeded-PPR flag gating occurs inside build/expand logic after module initialization.

focus: correctness finer grain: seeded-PPR deadline duplicate trace behavior; initial checklist evidence replay

findingsNew.title: Seeded-PPR trace output loses the multi-hop provenance chain
findingsNew.claim: When SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_SEEDED_PPR_RANKING and includeTrace are both enabled, multi-hop impact candidates are returned with depth from PPR, but why_included.edgeChain records only the candidate's final incoming edge, not the full path back to the anchor.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether WeightedWalkResult carries predecessor/path data and whether recovered PPR tests cover includeTrace for multi-hop ranking; neither was present.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: edgeChain may be intended as a set of contributing edges rather than an ordered path, but a single final edge cannot explain depth greater than 1 for a PPR candidate.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the trace contract documents seeded-PPR as single-edge-only or if downstream consumers ignore why_included for PPR impact mode.

findingsNew.title: Checklist completion evidence conflicts with the packet's own task state
findingsNew.claim: checklist.md declares all verification buckets complete, including P1 Items 10/10, while tasks.md and plan.md still leave live sync, packet doc updates, strict validation, and overall completion criteria unchecked.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Replayed strict spec validation, code-graph typecheck, and targeted seeded-PPR vitest commands; they pass, but passing commands do not reconcile the contradictory completion state across packet docs.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: tasks.md and plan.md may be stale while checklist.md is current, which is still a traceability defect because packet source-of-truth docs contradict completion evidence.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade after tasks.md/plan.md/checklist.md are reconciled to one completion state with command evidence.

findingDetails.title: Seeded-PPR trace output loses the multi-hop provenance chain

spec_code.notes: Seeded-PPR impact path remains gated and targeted tests pass, but includeTrace provenance is incomplete for multi-hop PPR candidates.

checklist_evidence.notes: Runnable commands pass, but checklist completion totals conflict with tasks.md and plan.md unchecked completion items.

focus: security/reliability: eval harness filesystem cleanup, env handling, and edge-confidence write paths

findingsNew.title: Eval harness cleanup is not failure-safe
findingsNew.claim: score-seeded-ppr-retrieval.mjs writes a generated child module into scripts/eval/ and creates copied DB work directories, but cleanup is only on the successful linear path; failures before final cleanup can leave ppr-impact-child.mjs or vitest-tmp/ppr-eval-* artifacts behind in the repo tree.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked for shell execution and untrusted path construction: spawnSync uses process.execPath with an argv array and no shell, topology values come from the local TOPOLOGIES object, and DB writes use prepared statements against the copied DB.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: This is an operator eval harness with fixed repo-local paths, so impact is reliability/worktree hygiene rather than injection or traversal.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if cleanup is wrapped in try/finally for both workDir and CHILD, or the generated child source is moved into the per-run temp directory and cleaned with it.

findingDetails.title: Eval harness cleanup is not failure-safe
findingDetails.scopeProof: Grep over the reviewed script's filesystem operations found only mkdtempSync/copyFileSync/writeFileSync/rmSync in this eval harness; structural-indexer.ts and cross-file-edge-resolver.ts reviewed write paths use fixed prepared SQL updates, not filesystem cleanup.

findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The team may intentionally avoid promoting default-off experimental flags after a CUT verdict, but feature catalogs and manual playbooks are capability/validation inventories and are the expected traceability surfaces for gated behavior.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to no finding if CUT/default-off experimental branches are intentionally excluded from catalogs/playbooks by policy, or if a sibling manual scenario already covers code_graph_context with both gated flags enabled.

findingDetails.title: Catalog and playbook omit the new gated code_graph_context capability

focus: Full line-by-line correctness audit of resolveCrossFileCallEdges confidence-differentiation write path

findingsNew.title: Name-only cross-file resolution is promoted to extracted confidence
findingsNew.claim: With SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_EDGE_CONFIDENCE_DIFFERENTIATION=true, the resolver assigns confidence 0.9 and evidenceClass EXTRACTED when exactly one same-name callable exists outside the importing file, even though it does not validate that the candidate came from the import declaration's target module.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked for selected import specifier or resolved module path validation in the resolver query and candidate filter; none is present. Checked focused cross-file resolver tests for a single-candidate import-target mismatch; existing cases cover happy path, ambiguity, enabled ambiguous metadata, same-file stability, and fixture-name disambiguation, but not this mismatch.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: Global name uniqueness may be the intended target-rewrite heuristic. Even if so, the enabled confidence metadata should remain heuristic/inferred unless the import target path is validated.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if another invariant guarantees import nodes only exist for scanned concrete module targets, or if downstream seeded-PPR ignores resolver-written confidence and evidenceClass metadata.

findingDetails.title: Name-only cross-file resolution is promoted to extracted confidence
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed resolveCrossFileCallEdges lines 84-169, edge confidence flag lines 5-11, edge metadata type lines 41-49, and focused cross-file resolver tests lines 132-392. The enabled resolved branch writes 0.9/EXTRACTED after name-only candidate selection; no import-target path check appears in the resolver.

traceabilityChecks.flagGating: PASS: metadata writes are inside confidenceDifferentiationEnabled branches; flag-off resolved path calls updateTarget only and ambiguous path does not write metadata.
traceabilityChecks.sqlParameterization: PASS: updateTarget, updateMetadata, and updateTargetAndMetadata use positional placeholders for all dynamic values.
traceabilityChecks.classificationBranches: PASS_WITH_NOTE: zero/one/many branches update unresolved/resolved/ambiguousSkipped consistently, but the one-candidate branch relies on global name uniqueness rather than import target path validation.
traceabilityChecks.candidateEdgeCases: NEW_FINDING: a single candidate in a different module than the import declaration can be assigned 0.9/EXTRACTED silently.

focus: Traceability: internal doc-to-doc consistency across packet docs

findingsNew.title: Verification docs conflate suite green with a known failing baseline
findingsNew.claim: The same verification fact is stated two incompatible ways: acceptance language says the existing code-graph suite should be green/passing with the new flag off, while completion evidence says the accepted result was a failing baseline of 6 failed files / 9 failed tests, later 5 failed files / 8 failed tests after the ADR-001 fix.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: The packet repeatedly frames the failed counts as pre-existing and says the invariant is zero new regressions, not absolute suite success. That supports the implementation result, but it does not reconcile the words passes and green in acceptance surfaces.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The authors may be using green colloquially to mean no new failures relative to baseline.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if every acceptance/checklist use of passes or green is rewritten to a baseline-relative formulation such as no new failures against the known failing baseline.

findingsNew.title: Spec references the benchmark record with a different relative path than the completion docs
findingsNew.claim: The original benchmark record path is stated differently across packet docs, and the spec.md variant points one directory too shallow.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: A sibling 007-dark-flag-graduation under 002-code-graph would make the spec path valid, but no such benchmark result path exists.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The spec was drafted before the packet hierarchy was finalized and later tasks/summary paths were corrected after sync.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to no finding if the spec path is corrected to ../../007-dark-flag-graduation/005-codegraph-seeded-ppr/benchmark-results.md or the missing target is intentionally created.

findingsNew.title: ADR-001 near-miss attribution points to the wrong packet in several docs
findingsNew.claim: The ADR-001 near-miss is described as violating this packet's own ADR-001 in task/checklist/summary docs, but the packet's decision record says the relevant second-walker ADR-001 lives in ../005-seeded-ppr-ranking/decision-record.md; this packet's ADR-001 is about reusing the discarded resolution-quality signal.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: A local ADR in this packet forbidding a second walker would reconcile the wording, but the current packet's ADR-001 text does not contain that decision.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The docs use this packet loosely to refer to the broader seeded-PPR revisit chain rather than this exact folder.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to no finding if tasks/checklist/summary clarify that the violated ADR was ../005-seeded-ppr-ranking/decision-record.md ADR-001, or if this packet adds an explicit local ADR cross-reference for the second-walker rule.

focus: Independent re-verification of seeded-PPR re-benchmark evidence

findingsNew.title: Confidence-distribution claim is not backed by the persisted raw benchmark evidence
findingsNew.claim: The revisit evidence says a fresh full-repo reindex produced four live CALLS-edge confidence tiers, but the persisted raw benchmark artifact does not contain that distribution, and the only local live code-graph database currently has a single 0.8 confidence tier.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Searched for additional results artifacts; only metrics.json exists. Queried the liveDbPath recorded in metrics.json; it was unavailable. Queried the current workspace code-graph DB; it returned only confidence 0.8 with 19487 CALLS edges. Compared changelog and benchmark prose for the claimed counts.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The four-tier distribution may have existed only in a temporary flag-on implementation worktree and may not have been intended to remain in the default-off production DB. If so, the docs need durable raw evidence or clearer qualification that the distribution is transient operator evidence, not recoverable benchmark output.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade or close if a durable raw artifact is added or referenced that contains the exact four confidence buckets and counts for the same benchmark run, or if the docs are amended to limit the claim to non-persisted operator evidence.

findingDetails.title: Confidence-distribution claim is not backed by the persisted raw benchmark evidence
findingDetails.scopeProof: Glob for results/** under the seeded-PPR benchmark folder returned only results/metrics.json; metrics.json contains aggregates but no confidence histogram; sqlite query on the only local code-graph.sqlite returned 0.8|19487 rather than the claimed four-tier distribution.

findingsNew.title: Full-suite verification count no longer matches the packet's claimed baseline

findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The broader suite may be sensitive to runtime state, cwd resolution, concurrent daemon state, or unrelated drift; that may explain the failures, but it still contradicts the packet's unqualified reproducible baseline-count claim in the current tree.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade or close if a rerun in the documented expected environment produces the claimed baseline counts, or if the docs are amended to state the current reproducible baseline and environmental preconditions.

focus: Confidence-value internal consistency audit for edge metadata consumers

findingsNew.title: AMBIGUOUS evidence no longer sets why_included.ambiguous
findingsNew.claim: The trace consumer still derives why_included[].ambiguous with formattedEdge.evidenceClass === 'INFERRED', so newly differentiated CALLS edges marked AMBIGUOUS are emitted with an AMBIGUOUS edge-chain step but the containing file-level trace entry is not marked ambiguous.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked contextEdgeReliability and confirmed AMBIGUOUS is intentionally ranked lower than INFERRED, so ranking is not the broken consumer. Checked query-result-adapter and found pass-through behavior only, with no ambiguity reinterpretation.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The field could have been intended to mean only inferred, not ambiguous. The nearby comment and field name contradict that narrower reading, and the new producer emits an explicit AMBIGUOUS class.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the public contract is clarified so why_included.ambiguous deliberately means only INFERRED, or if includeTrace consumers rely solely on edgeChain[].evidenceClass.

findingDetails.title: AMBIGUOUS evidence no longer sets why_included.ambiguous
findingDetails.scopeProof: Scoped grep across mcp_server/lib for EdgeEvidenceClass|evidenceClass|contextEdgeReliability|\.confidence\b|confidence: found the rank-blend consumer, the trace consumer, the adapter pass-through, and producer sites; no other in-scope consumer reinterprets AMBIGUOUS as non-ambiguous.

traceabilityChecks.edgeEvidenceTypeCoverage: pass: CodeEdgeMetadata evidenceClass is exactly EXTRACTED|INFERRED|AMBIGUOUS in indexer-types.ts
traceabilityChecks.sharedPayloadVocabulary: not_applicable: shared-payload GraphEdgeEnrichment uses a separate edgeEvidenceClass vocabulary and is not the CodeEdgeMetadata path
traceabilityChecks.contextEdgeReliabilityRange: pass: clampConfidence accepts 0..1 and the four values are in range
traceabilityChecks.contextEdgeReliabilityDistribution: pass_with_caveat: rank factors already distinguish EXTRACTED, INFERRED, and AMBIGUOUS with no >=0.5 assumption
traceabilityChecks.otherConsumers: conditional: query adapter passes through safely; why_included trace ambiguity mishandles AMBIGUOUS

focus: Rollback and flag-toggle correctness for code graph edge-confidence differentiation and seeded-PPR ranking

findingsNew.title: Flag-off impact queries still consume differentiated confidence persisted while the flag was on
findingsNew.claim: The flag values are read fresh on the observed write and seeded-PPR query paths, but rollback is not behaviorally clean. After an operator enables SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_EDGE_CONFIDENCE_DIFFERENTIATION, scans, then disables it, flag-off impact paths still rank, display, and in one blast-radius path filter by differentiated metadata.confidence and metadata.evidenceClass left in code_edges.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether the seeded-PPR gate itself was cached or whether the impact branch normalized confidence when the edge-confidence flag is off. seededPprRankingEnabled reads process.env at call time and shouldUseSeededPprRanking is called inside the impact branch, but no equivalent edge-confidence read or normalization appears in rankContextEdges, contextEdgeReliability, formatContextEdge, or parseEdgeMetadataConfidence.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The team may intend persisted confidence to become canonical once written, independent of the runtime flag. That conflicts with this iteration's stated rollback contract that the flag-off path behave as if the scan had never happened with the flag on.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 or no finding if the intended product contract is changed so SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_EDGE_CONFIDENCE_DIFFERENTIATION gates writes only, and previously persisted confidence metadata is explicitly allowed to affect flag-off reads.

findingDetails.title: Flag-off impact queries still consume differentiated confidence persisted while the flag was on
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same stale metadata is consumed by code_graph_context rank/output code and by the blast-radius dependency confidence filter; both are read/query surfaces rather than write paths.

focus: final gap-check and risk-ranking synthesis

findingsNew.title: AMBIGUOUS evidence can be classified as direct-call evidence outside code_graph_context
findingsNew.claim: Relationship-query and scan enrichment consumers only classify metadata.evidenceClass === 'INFERRED' as weak; AMBIGUOUS falls through to direct_call unless detectorProvenance is also present and set to heuristic. Cross-file confidence differentiation can write evidenceClass: 'AMBIGUOUS' without guaranteeing detectorProvenance, so older/null-metadata edges can be mislabeled as direct evidence.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked the main same-file producer and found structural-indexer ambiguous CALLS metadata is built with detectorProvenance: 'heuristic', so those edges are classified weakly despite the missing AMBIGUOUS branch. Checked cross-file resolver update logic and found it preserves existing metadata but only guarantees confidence and evidenceClass on write, leaving old/null metadata without guaranteed provenance.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The current normal reindex path likely gives most CALLS edges provenance metadata, so the bug may only surface on older DBs, partial migrations, manually inserted rows, or rows whose metadata was previously null.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 or close if the supported deployment contract requires a full fresh reindex before enabling edge-confidence differentiation and tests prove all AMBIGUOUS-producing write paths always carry detectorProvenance: 'heuristic' before any relationship or scan enrichment consumer reads them.

findingDetails.title: AMBIGUOUS evidence can be classified as direct-call evidence outside code_graph_context
findingDetails.scopeProof: Searched mcp_server for evidenceClass/EdgeEvidenceClass/ambiguous consumers. Prior iteration 18 covered mcp_server/lib; this final pass checked handlers/query.ts and handlers/scan.ts and found the same INFERRED-only predicate outside that scope. Structural-indexer normally supplies heuristic provenance, but cross-file-edge-resolver only guarantees confidence and evidenceClass on metadata rewrite.

traceabilityChecks.seededPprFlagOffStaleness: No analogous persisted PPR cache/trace/telemetry state found; seeded-PPR maps are request-local and the legacy path is used when shouldUseSeededPprRanking is false.
traceabilityChecks.evidenceClassConsumers: New P1 found outside the prior mcp_server/lib scope in handlers/query.ts and handlers/scan.ts.
traceabilityChecks.topLevelImportPattern: No second startup-blocking top-level dynamic import found in changed production files; other production dynamic imports are function-scoped.

focus: final gap-check and risk-ranking synthesis
